jd_text: Job Purpose: To lead and oversee all operational aspects of the entertainment division, ensuring exceptional guest experiences, operational excellence, and strong financial performance. The role is responsible for pre-opening setup, strategic planning, and ongoing management of entertainment venues, including F&B, leisure, and large-scale guest experiences. 1. Main Duties & Responsibilities: Lead the overall operations of the entertainment unit, ensuring alignment with business goals and brand standards. Drive revenue growth, profitability, and operational efficiency across all entertainment and F&B outlets. Oversee pre-opening activities including planning, hiring, training, SOP development, and operational setup. Ensure seamless guest experience by maintaining high service standards across all touchpoints. Develop and implement operational strategies, policies, and procedures to improve performance and efficiency. Manage and mentor department heads and operational teams to ensure high performance and engagement. Work closely with Marketing, Sales, and PR teams to drive footfall, campaigns, and brand visibility. Monitor financial performance including budgets, forecasts, cost control, and revenue optimization. Ensure compliance with health, safety, and regulatory requirements. Oversee event execution, activations, and entertainment programming to enhance guest engagement. Build strong relationships with stakeholders, vendors, and partners. Identify opportunities for innovation, new concepts, and continuous improvement in operations. Prepare and present regular reports on operational performance, KPIs, and strategic initiatives. 2. Pre-Opening Responsibilities: Lead the full pre-opening phase of entertainment venues (e.g., Topgolf or similar concepts). Establish operational structures, staffing plans, and training programs. Coordinate with project teams for facility readiness and operational handover. Implement SOPs, service standards, and operational workflows. Ensure successful launch and opening of the venue within timelines and budget. 3. Qualifications & Experience: Minimum Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or related field. 8–12+ years of experience in hospitality, entertainment, or leisure operations. Proven experience in leadership roles managing large teams and multi-unit operations. Preferred Experience: Experience in pre-opening projects (Topgolf, entertainment venues, F&B concepts, resorts). Strong background in entertainment, leisure, or lifestyle-driven hospitality brands. 4. Key Skills: Strong leadership and team management skills Commercial and financial acumen Excellent communication and stakeholder management Strategic thinking and problem-solving ability Operational excellence and attention to detail Ability to work in a fast-paced, high-pressure environment
